It is a place of brooding mountains, of Great Plains and whispering lakes - a place that attracts a diversity of life to its wonders - and it was once believed to be the place of the birthplace of consciousness in mankind. Eight lakes make up the Kenya Rift Valley Lake system, two of which are freshwater and the rest alkaline. The large flocks of flamingoes for which the Kenyan lakes have become famous are found on the alkaline soda lakes feeding on tiny crustaceans
itinerary: Nairobi:: We pick you up at the International Airport in Nairobi and accompany you to the hotel. Spend the rest of the day relaxing and getting ready to start your Africa adventure.

Nairobi:: Your safari starts at Magadi Lake. It is the southernmost of the great lakes of Kenya and is located 80 km from Nairobi. We will pick you up at the hotel in Nairobi after breakfast and drive towards the Tanzanian border: past the famous Ngong mountains (also known as the film "Out of Africa") into the Great Rift Valley. Enroute is Olorgesaille one of the most famous archaeological sites in East Africa where archaeologists Louis and Mary Leakey discovered not only the fossils of the early methods of man but also the world's largest collection of fist wedges and hand axes. Accompanied by your guide tour the prehistoric site and learn how the early hominids have developed in the savannahs of Africa and why their fossil remains have been preserved over millions of years in the sediment of the lakes. Afterwards, drive to Magadi and have lunch picnics at the hot springs of Soda ash. On the lakeside, you will experience flamingo colonies, pelicans, goshawks and other water birds. Then cross the lake on the road to Ol 'Kiramatian and learn how the salt mixtures are mined at the lake. On the other side, you climb up the adjoining mountain slopes on Magadi Lake. From above you can enjoy a fantastic view of the entire lake and parts of the moat and walk along the rocky slopes. Our local guide is part of the Maasai tribe and will tell us about the traditions and ways of life of the local Masai people. In the late afternoon, return to Nairobi. In the hotel, you can have dinner and relax from the day.

Naivasha:: Today you drive to Naivasha, the 2nd Rift Valley Lake on your tour, where you will be taken to Crescent Island. The tour usually takes 2 hours and you can explore both the lake and the island with all the birds and animals. Once you arrive at the island, you will meet the guide near the pier. Your guide will show you the best animals: giraffes, hippos, Grant's gazelles, the fox and maybe even the rare pythons. The boat will take you back to Lake Naivasha Country Club, where you will have lunch. The rest of the day is at leisure.

Lake Elmenteita:: Today you will continue to Lake Elementaita where you will take a hike to "Sleeping Warrior", a hill that has the profile of a sleeping Maasai warrior. On the shore you can watch pelicans.

Nakuru:: If you want, you can watch the birds again in the early morning at the lakeside. After breakfast, we continue to Lake Nakuru National Park, where you will arrive at the Flamingo Hills Camp at noon. Check in and have lunch. In the afternoon you will be on a game drive. The Lake Nakuru National Park was created to protect the huge flamingo population. On the bank, waterbucks, Bohor-Reedbuck and zebras wander around grazing. Lake Nakuru is home to the black and white rhinoceros, In 1987 only two black rhinoceroses had remained because of the devastating effects of poaching and by establishing a rhinoceros-protected area within the park and crossing with a breeder from Laikipia, the Kenya Wildlife Service (K.W.S.) succeeded in multiplying rhinoceros successfully in the park. Wildlife observation is relatively easy and will be possible until sunset. Buffalo, Leopard, Lion, Rothschild giraffe, and Colobus can be found in the forest.

Lake Baringo National Park:: Lake Bogoria National Reserves is 107 km² in size. Here you can find hot springs and geysers in the once wild and repellent landscape, which is permeated by dark lava rocks. Or you can observe the masses of flamingos at Lake Bogoria, which move in a row, while the head of the algae is beaked with the head down. There are also bee-eaters, kingfishers, rhino-birds and many other bird species among the feathered inhabitants. There are also some mammals, such as the large herds of the Great Kudus, an antelope that is otherwise very rare in East Africa, but also buffaloes, zebras, dik-diks, impalas, warthogs, and baboons.

Lake Baringo National Park:: After breakfast, you will have beautiful views of the lake and the islands in the changing light of the sunrise. Lake Baringo is the second freshwater lake in the Rift Valley after Lake Naivasha and is the highest at 970 m above sea level. There are over 450 species of birds in and around the lake area. Crocodiles and hippos are also frequently spotted with the latter coming out at night in many places to graze ashore. Here is one of the best places to photograph Squacco heron, Goliath heron and purple heron. On the lake are local fishermen who paddle around in their balsa wood boats and fish Tilapia. Perhaps you would like to experience a special spectacle and buy a fish from one of the fishermen, then look out for African fish Eagles, which are usually found in pairs on the banks and on the islands. When you have tracked the big birds, the fisherman will raise the fish from the boat - and the fish eagle will try to catch the fish.

Kerio Valley National Park:: Today you drive over the Rift to Kabernet and continue to the Chebloch Gorge, where you can linger and watch cliff jumpers. Drive through the Kerio Valley through breathtaking landscapes and traditional Pokot villages to the Rimoi Nation Reserve, where you can observe elephants migrating between Turkana and Kerio Valley. From here, we continue uphill to Iten, the city of marathon runners. At the Kerio View Hotel you have a breathtaking view over the valley and can even paraglide.
Kenya:: Continue your journey north to Kitale at the foot of Mt. Elgon. You can visit the mountain or participate in a coffee tasting at the Cran es Camp, which has its own coffee plantation.

Lake Turkana:: In the morning leave for the Airstrip for your flight to Lodwar. There we take you from the airfield and drive directly to Eliye Springs on the shores of Lake Turkana (approximately one hour). The largest desert lake in the world is considered the cradle of mankind because archaeologists discovered some of the most spectacular and ancient hominid remains. At the Eliye Springs Resort, you will find the Boma, a hotel banda, which is modelled after the traditional straw huts of Turkana, the local tribe. Get familiar with your hotel, and enjoy the first hours at the beach. In the late afternoon, visit the nearby fishing village of Longelech and learn how the Turkana operate traditional "shore" -fishing, and river fishing. The fishermen prepare freshly caught fish on request at the beach.

Lake Turkana:: After breakfast, you will drive with the jeep to the north of the lake. On the sand slopes, you will travel through nomads to Kalokol, the centre of the Turkana fishermen on the lake. In the bay of Kalokol, Nile perches and tilapias spawn, and walk through one of the traditional villages of Turkana. Visit a boma and learn how the Turkana make their diverse body jewellery and experience the way of life of the nomads. In Nariokotome you visit the old Spanish mission station with its gardens and plants. Many of the locals still speak Spanish. Therefore Nariokotome is also paraphrased as "Little Spain", The place, however, gained its fame only by the paleoanthropologist Richard Leakey. The team discovered the almost complete skeleton of a 1.6 million-year-old prehistoric species Homo erectus in the Nariokotome River in 1984 near the lake shore. The bone remnants came from a boy about nine years old. Visit the monument with the replica of the Turkana boy and visit the excavation site.

Lake Turkana:: In the middle of Lake Turkana, there are three islands of volcanic origin. After breakfast, take a motorboat across to the middle island. Central Iceland is designated as a national park. The three crater lakes on the island "Crocodile Lake", "Flamingo Lake" and "Tilapia Lake" are the most important breeding grounds of Crocodylus niloticus, the Nile Crocodile, walk between the crater lakes and discover the island.

Lake Turkana:: On the last day, you can enjoy the beach, lake and sun. On request, guests can enjoy a massage at the resort or a fishing trip. In the afternoon you will go to the last highlight of your trip- a boat trip with a Sundowner.
End of Itinerary:: Transfer to Lodwar to the Airstrip for your flight to Nairobi from where you will catch your international flight home. Please enter your email address and/or telephone number.

touren.name: 10 Days Western Kenya Adventure
touren.desc: Embark on a captivating 10-day journey through Kenya, starting with a city stay at Trademark Hotel in Nairobi. The adventure takes you to the renowned Maasai Mara Game Reserve, where you'll witness the incredible wildlife, including prides of lions and the annual wildebeest migration. Following the Mara, travel to Mbita on Lake Victoria, enjoying a scenic drive through the Rift Valley. Explore Ruma National Park, known for its efforts in conserving the endangered Roan antelope, and experience the cultural richness of Kisumu, including Lake Simbi Nyaima and the Duga Wetlands. Venture to Kakamega Forest for nature walks and bird watching. Game drives in Lake Nakuru National Park and a boat ride on Lake Naivasha to Crescent Island, where you walk among giraffes and zebras.  Conclude the journey with a return to Nairobi, marking the end of this diverse and enriching safari experience.
itinerary: Nairobi:: Upon arrival at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, you will be met by our representative for transfer to your city hotel where you can relax. Meals at own cost.

Masai Mara:: After an early breakfast we travel to Maasai Mara Game Reserve, so named to mean the “spotted plains” in Maa language. This is home to the greatest concentration of large mammals and is widely recognized as Africa’s ultimate wildlife reserve. The park is the northern extension of the vast Serengeti-Mara ecosystem, famous for its annual migration of wildebeest. Transfer to the lodge with a game drive en route and after check-in enjoy a hot lunch. Later in the evening enjoy a game drive in this spectacular place of breathtaking scenery, never-ending plains and the most abundant variety of wildlife remaining anywhere in the world. The landscape is made up of rolling savannahs dotted with patchy shrubs and bush thickets. This afternoon, visit the Masai Beadwork Museum to admire the beauty of this ancient craft and learn about the lore and meanings communicated by each piece. You will also visit a traditional Masai village to learn more about their culture and daily life.

Masai Mara:: During your stay, you have the opportunity to explore this great reserve in depth. You may see elephants, giraffes, prides of lions, and thousands of impalas, topi, grants, and Thomson's gazelle. Birds are prolific including immigrants, 450 bird species have been recorded among them 57 birds of prey. The Mara is the scene of the annual migration of millions of wildebeest up from the Serengeti plains in search of water and grasses. Monring and afternoon game drives in Masai Mara

Lake Victoria, Kenya:: Depart after early breakfast for a long drive via Bomet/Sotik to Mbita on Lake Victoria. This drive will take you across the floor of the Rift Valley and over the Ololoolo escarpment into the fertile Western highlands rich with tea and coffee plantations. Enjoy your picnic lunch en-route. Check in and spend the rest of the evening at leisure within the lodge grounds, enjoying the beautiful sundowner over Africa’s largest freshwater lake. Optionally, you can book a boat ride on Lake Victoria - Africa's largest lake! (At Extra cost).

Lake Victoria, Kenya:: Set off on an unforgettable day trip to Kenya’s hidden gem, Ruma National Park. After a hearty breakfast and a short drive across scenic countryside, they enter the park’s tranquil landscapes of rolling savannah, acacia woodlands, and dramatic escarpments. Accompanied by an expert guide, the game drive reveals Ruma’s special wildlife: elegant roan antelopes (one of Kenya’s last strongholds for the species), majestic giraffes, buffalo, zebra, and a rich variety of birdlife. Lucky visitors may even spot elusive leopards or the park’s healthy population of lions resting in the shade. As the golden afternoon light bathes the valley, the group makes their way back to Victoria Sands.

Kisumu:: After breakfast, you continue to Lake Simbi. Lake Simbi Nyaima offers a breathtaking legendary tale, Simbi Nyaima” means the village that sank; it is a seasonal home of migrating flamingos. They arrive from lakes in the Rift Valley region, such as Nakuru, Baringo, Natron and Elementaita every year. You will arrive for lunch at Kiboko Bay and check in your room. In the afternoon, you will go on an excursion to the Duga Wetland, which is a haven for many species of birdlife and is also home to rare Sitatunga antelope. You will also enjoy a walk on the boardwalk above the marsh to take in the views. Lake Simbi and Duga Wetlands visit

Kisumu:: Today we will drive an hour away from Kisumu to the last remaining rainforest in Kenya, Kakamega Forest. There are hiking trails in the forest that allow for forest walking, camping, hiking, primate watching, bird and butterfly watching, game watching and village walks. In the afternoon, you return to Kisumu and can have an optional sunset cruise at the lake.

Naivasha:: Today, you will journey to the enchanting Lake Nakuru National Park, arriving around midday. Nestled within the Great Rift Valley, this park is famed for its vast flocks of pink flamingos that once transformed the lake into a shimmering sea of color. Although the water level is now higher due to climate change, leading to fewer flamingos, the park still offers incredible wildlife experiences—especially the chance to see one of the endangered Big Five: the magnificent rhinoceros. After checking in, you’ll enjoy a delicious lunch before setting out on an exciting afternoon game drive. Along the lake’s edge, you may spot waterbucks, reedbucks, and graceful zebras. Lake Nakuru is home to both black and white rhinoceroses. The story of the black rhino is particularly moving: in 1987, only two individuals remained in the wild. Thanks to the establishment of a rhino sanctuary within the park and a successful breeding program with a partner from Laikipia, the Kenya Wildlife Service managed to bring the population back from the brink of extinction.

Naivasha:: After breakfast and check-out, you set off for Lake Naivasha, the first of the Rift Valley lakes, nestled at the foot of the majestic Mount Longonot. Lake Naivasha holds historical significance as the landing site of East Africa’s first air passengers, who arrived here aboard Imperial Airways’ flying boats. Following a leisurely lunch at the camp, you will embark on a charming afternoon boat ride across the lake. This is a perfect opportunity to observe the diverse waterbird species and spot hippos in their natural habitat.
End of Itinerary:: After an early breakfast it´s time to depart to Nairobi. Your Safari ends here. Transfer to Jomo Kenyatta International Airport

touren.name: 08 Days Kenya Bush and North Coast
touren.desc: This is the perfect combination of beach and bush! Enjoy the dramatic contrasts of the open plains bordered by undulating hills of the vast Tsavo East National Park, home to the famed “man eaters” lions made famous in the movie “the ghost & the darkness” to the dazzling Indian Ocean Coast!
itinerary: Diani Beach:: Arrival in Mombasa in the morning and transfer to Watamu. Relax at the beach. Watamu is a beautiful stretch of sandy white beaches bordering the Indian Ocean. Enjoy the laidback beach vibes of this small town, known as an eco-tourism and watersports hub.

Tsavo West National Park:: Drive to Tsavo East National Park. Arrive for lunch followed by an early evening game drive to return back to lodge/camp after sunset. Tsavos’s abundant wildlife awaits especially the the sight of dust-red elephants who appear red from the mineral-rich earth they spray on themselves. The sight of these gentle giants wallowing, rolling, and spraying each other with the waters of the palm-shaded Galana River is one of the most evocative images in Africa. This along with the 300-kilometer-long Yatta Plateau, the longest lava flow in the world, makes for an adventure unlike any other. The park forms the largest protected area in Kenya and is home to most of the larger mammals, vast herds of elephant, Rhino, buffalo, lion, leopard, pods of hippo, crocodile, waterbucks, lesser Kudu, gerenuk, and the prolific bird life features 500 recorded species.

Tsavo West National Park:: Head out on another exciting day exploring Tsavo East in search of the park's famous wildlife. Seek out prides of lions which may be the descendants of the famous pair or man-eating lions of the 19th century. They are usually found close to the huge herds of buffalo which call the park home. Return to the lodge for a refreshing sundowner and admire the spectacular colours of the sunset.

Diani Beach:: After breakfast, you will drive back to Kenya´s coast. You will spend the next 4 nights here. Stroll along the soft white sands, take a cool dip in the pool, relax on the beach, watch the sunrise & sit back and relax. Watamu is known for Watamu Marine National Park and Reserve, which has 3 bays: Watamu, Blue Lagoon and Turtle Lagoon. This is one of the best places in East Africa to enjoy snorkelling and diving. Sandy beaches and coral gardens are the defining features. Seabirds, as well as green and hawksbill turtles, inhabit Mida Creek with its sand flats and mangrove forest.

Diani Beach:: Optional Excursion 1 – Malindi & Gede** Be picked up from your hotel by your guide. You will then be taken to Malindi to view the historic buildings and attractions, namely the Jumaa mosque, village homes, shops, and the Palace. Visit the Vasco da Gama pillar, one of the oldest European monuments in Africa and learn about Vasco da Gama. You will then be taken to experience the beauty of the Mida Creek, 12 square miles of Mangrove forests. The creek makes an incredible transition from the coral reefs of the ocean creating a sanctuary for many species of wild birds such as sand plovers, egrets, terns, whimbrels, curlew sandpipers and sanderlings. From there, transfer to visit the remains of a Swahili town called Gedi. Here your guide will tell you about the history of the palace and tombs and the spectacular variety of trees including combretum, tamarind, baobab, wild Ficus and sterculia. Visit the Watamu Snake Farm where the largest collection of snakes in East Africa is found. Drive back to your hotel.

Diani Beach:: Optional Excursion 2 – Sundowner at Hell’s Kitchen** A 30-minute drive from Malindi town is a small unassuming village with conspicuous baobab trees. The village is called Marafa, but its proximity to the biodiversity rich Dakatcha woodlands is not its most outstanding feature. That title goes to the Marafa depression, more famously known as Hell’s Kitchen. Hell’s Kitchen is an odd sandstone canyon that might be Kenya’s best kept secret. The mini canyon is officially known as Hell’s Kitchen. The name comes from the high temperatures for which it is famous. No tours are done during the day as the temperatures reach highs of 50 degrees at some point in the canyon. But we will invite you for a sundowner to this amazing place.
Diani Beach:: Spend the day at leisure, perhaps relaxing on the beach or by the swimming pool. Stroll along the beach and have lunch at a local cafe. Spend time soaking up laid-back beach life.

End of Itinerary:: There may be time for one last dip in the warm waters of the Indian Ocean before we say goodbye. We will drop you in Mombasa for your flight home. Please enter your email address and/or telephone number.

touren.name: 08 Days Bush and Beach Diani and Tsavo
touren.desc: This is the perfect combination of beach and bush! Enjoy the dramatic contrasts of the open plains bordered by undulating hills of the vast Tsavo East National Park, home to the famed “man eaters” lions made famous in the movie “the ghost & the darkness” to the dazzling Indian Ocean Coast! This Safari offers both adventures by day and unabashed luxury at night - this is pure African romance under the stars
itinerary: Diani Beach:: Upon arrival in Kenya, you will be met by our customer service representative who will then assist you to your vehicle for the drive to your beach hotel. Upon arrival enjoy a nonalcoholic welcome drink followed by a short briefing session on your vacation. You can then enjoy the resort or relax on the beach, enjoying a dip in the warm waters of the Indian Ocean.

Ngutuni Wildlife Sanctuary:: Today we will explore Ngutuni Sanctury / Tsavo East National Park, which together with Tsavo West forms Kenya's largest national park covering almost 22 000 km². Named after the Tsavo River, which flows from west to east, the park is considered one of the strongholds of biodiversity in the world. It is a huge park and guests often have to exercise patience during game viewing, but the rewards are great. The experienced safari guides are familiar with game movements and the seasonal migration routes and times of wildlife in Tsavo. Some of Tsavo East's most popular attractions are Mudanda Rock, Yatta Plateau and Lugard Falls.

Ngutuni Wildlife Sanctuary:: After breakfast, you will spend the whole day exploring Tsavo on game drives in the park with a picnic lunch stop. The park is famous for its huge herds of elephants which often have a reddish appearance due to their habit of dust bathing in the reddish iron-rich earth that characterises the local area.

Diani Beach:: Today, we return to the beach. Depart after breakfast to the coast and spend your day relaxing with various activities at your beach hotel such as enjoying a relaxing massage. If you are feeling more adventurous, book a kite surfing lesson or head out to explore the local reefs on a snorkelling excursion. If you are of a more gentle persuasion, perhaps a boat ride through the nearby mangrove forest to discover all the creatures that call it home may be of interest.

Diani Beach:: Optional Excursion: WASINI ISLAND** Pick up at your hotel at 07.00 am and drive to the historical small fishing village of Shimoni on the South Coast. You will drive through rural villages, passing tall palm trees and lush greenery. On arrival, a local guide will lead you through the Shimoni caves where slaves were “stored” during the old slave trading days before shipment to the Zanzibar slave market. Then you board a traditional Arab Dhow for a cruise to the Marine Park. As you sail out on the Indian Ocean, there is an approximately 90% chance to spot Dolphins. You snorkel and marvel at the rich marine life where corals, exotic fish and sea plants are protected. You are provided with masks and snorkels and are accompanied by a certified and experienced guide. Assistance is also provided to first-timers. Back on board, you can refresh yourself with fruits and soft drinks. Sail to Wasini Island, where a tropical seafood lunch is waiting for you. The four-course menu includes crab, fish, seaweed, and rice with coconut sauce. You can also opt for a chicken or vegetarian menu. Beer, wine, and soft drinks are included. The restaurant, built on coral architecture and located directly at the shores, overlooks the channel towards Shimoni. After lunch, you have the option to take a discovery stroll through the Island and its Swahili culture, visiting the beautiful coral garden and the mangrove forest. Alternatively, you can relax at the restaurant, enjoying the sea view and the breeze. Return to your hotel around 5 pm. (all activities and fees included)

Diani Beach:: Spend another day enjoying all the resort has to offer such as jet skiing or kite surfing. Enjoy strolling on the beach, relaxing by the pool and enjoying cocktails from the bar. Take advantage of laid back coastal life!

End of Itinerary:: There may be time for one last morning refreshing dip in the warm waters of the Indian Ocean and breakfast before you check out and depart for the airport for your return flight home. Please enter your email address and/or telephone number.

touren.name: 04 Days Train Safari to Teita Estate
touren.desc: This 4 day train safari is for travelers who want to take it slow and enjoy the moment. Teita Estate and its own nature reserve are an insider tip far away from mass tourism and the hustle and bustle. With just a few guests, you can enjoy the tranquillity of nature.
itinerary: Kenya:: Today we drive you to the train station for the train to Voi and to Teita Hills to Hildana Lodge Hildana Lodge is located at the foot of the Taita Hills, nestled in a private 5,000-hectare conservancy. This small, exclusive property promises complete privacy and unfettered access to amazing wildlife and birding opportunities. We offer serenity and a luxurious escape from the hustle and bustle of reality, a home away from home in the African wilderness.

Kenya:: The lodge is adjacent to a beautiful, family-owned sisal estate. Therefore, we offer cozy accommodation that welcomes you with open arms and a smile from every member of the team. The sanctuary is part of the farm and is a testament to the family's passion and commitment to protecting wildlife and the surrounding environment. It is located in the heart of the Tsavo Elephant Corridor, which connects Lake Jipe in Tsavo West with the Galana River in Tsavo East. The Hildana waterholes are the seasonal home to large herds of elephants and other wildlife species and are the highlight of any visit to Hildana Lodge; here you can watch elephants quench their thirst in the evening as they travel through the reserve.

Kenya:: Discover the world of exciting activities at Hildana. Game drives and sundowners are included in the package. As well a farm tour. Hildana is te second largest farm in the world. More than 12.000 people live here. It´s one of the biggest sisal producer. The factory tour takes around 2 hours and is very interesting
End of Itinerary:: After breakfast transfer back to Voi train station for your train to Mombasa or Nairobi Please enter your email address and/or telephone number.

touren.name: 10 Days Luxury Rail Safari and Beach Holiday
touren.desc: FB+: Full Board Plus - Dinner, Bed, Breakfast, Lunch and Activities |  HB: Half Board - Dinner, Bed and Breakfast
itinerary: Kenya:: Your Kenya adventure begins with a warm welcome upon arrival at Moi International Airport in Mombasa, where your driver meets you and assists with a smooth transfer to the nearby Mombasa SGR Terminus. You board the Madaraka Express train bound for Voi SGR Station in the Tsavo region, enjoying a scenic 2- to 3-hour journey through the Kenyan countryside with views of rolling plains and distant wildlife. Upon arrival at Voi, a short road transfer of about 30-45 minutes brings you to The Hildana Lodge. Check in at this luxurious, intimate safari retreat nestled at the foot of the Taita Hills in a private 5,000-acre sanctuary that forms part of the vital Tsavo elephant corridor. After settling into your elegant en-suite cottage—complete with Mediterranean-Swahili style interiors and stunning views—you have time to unwind by the swimming pool or in the shared lounging areas while taking in the serene landscape. In the afternoon, you embark on a shared game drive in an open vehicle through the sanctuary, where you'll likely encounter impressive herds of elephants drawn to the seasonal waterholes (including the one right in front of the lodge), along with possibilities of spotting giraffes, zebras, antelopes, cheetahs, lions, or even the elusive African wild dog. The drive offers a thrilling introduction to the wildlife corridor linking Tsavo East and West, with expert guiding to highlight the area's rich biodiversity. Return in time for sundowners, followed by a delicious dinner under the stars, perhaps with views of nocturnal visitors at the waterhole, setting a magical tone for your safari experience.

Kenya:: Start with an early morning shared game drive, capitalizing on the best wildlife viewing times as the sun rises over the sanctuary. You'll explore different routes across the 5,000-acre private reserve, focusing on the elephant migration paths and water sources that attract large herds and other species like big cats and diverse birdlife, providing excellent photographic opportunities and insights into the ecosystem's conservation role. After returning for a hearty breakfast and some relaxation—perhaps lounging by the pool or enjoying the peaceful surroundings—you head out in the afternoon for the guided tour of the century-old sisal factory and working estate farm. This fascinating included experience offers a behind-the-scenes look at how the eco-friendly sisal plant is harvested, processed, and utilized, as well as the broader operations of the family-owned estate—the second-largest sisal producer in the world. It supports thousands of local families through sustainable agriculture, including arable projects, a fish farm, and hydroponic initiatives powered by factory wastewater. The tour blends history, industry, and community impact, providing a unique cultural contrast to the wildlife focus. The day concludes with a relaxed evening at the lodge, dinner, and perhaps more wildlife sightings from your vantage point. Tour of a working Sisal plantation

Kenya:: Enjoy wildlife encounters with both a morning and afternoon shared game drive in open vehicles. The early drive capitalizes on cooler temperatures and active animals, venturing across the sanctuary to track elephant herds at waterholes, predators like cheetahs or lions, diverse antelopes, giraffes, and rich birdlife in this vital Tsavo corridor. After returning for breakfast and a leisurely midday—perhaps pool time, relaxation in your cottage, or simply enjoying the views—you set out again in the afternoon for another game drive, exploring different areas or following fresh tracks for varied sightings and a chance at something special like a big cat hunt or large mammal gatherings. Optional add-ons (at extra cost) could include guided nature walks in nearby Taita Hills for endemic birds or intimate cultural encounters if desired. Evenings bring sundowners in the bush or by the pool, a memorable dinner, and the magical sight of elephants quenching their thirst at the lodge's waterhole under the African sky. Morning and afternoon game drives

Wasini Island:: After a final morning activity and breakfast, you check out and transfer back to Voi SGR Station to catch a return train to Mombasa Terminus. The comfortable rail ride provides more views of the Kenyan terrain. Upon reaching Mombasa, a private transfer of about 1 to 1.5 hours heads south along the coast to Diani Beach, where you arrive at Mwazaro Beach Lodge. This tranquil beachfront retreat features 300 meters of private, pristine beach backed by mangroves, away from busier areas, with sea-facing rooms built from natural materials and a laid-back coastal atmosphere. You check in, unwind on the white sands, take a swim in the warm Indian Ocean, and enjoy dinner as the sun sets.
Wasini Island:: Spend the day relaxing at the lodge or book an optional activity or excursion. Optional Wasini Island Exursion (extra cost)**
Wasini Island:: Spend another day relaxing and enjoying the Swahili Coast. Optional excursion (extra cost) Kaya Kinondo Forest Walk**
Wasini Island:: Spend another day relaxing and enjoying the beach.
Wasini Island:: Spend another day relaxing and enjoying your time at the beach. Optional excursion: Whale Shark spotting (extra cost - seasonal)**
Wasini Island:: Spend the day relaxing and enjoying your time at the Swahili Coast
End of Itinerary:: After breakfast, check out and transfer back to Moi International Airport in Mombasa for your onward travels. Please enter your email address and/or telephone number.

touren.name: 12 Days Western Kenya & Rift Valley
touren.desc: This captivating 12-day Kenya safari and cultural adventure begins in the vibrant capital of Nairobi and takes you on a journey through some of Kenya’s most diverse and breathtaking landscapes. From the tranquil shores of Lake Naivasha with its boat excursions and thrilling game drives in Hell’s Gate National Park, to the flamingo-filled soda lakes of Nakuru and Baringo, the itinerary offers a rich blend of wildlife, scenic beauty, and unique experiences. You’ll explore the dramatic Kerio Valley and Rimoi National Reserve, discover the lush biodiversity of Kakamega Forest, and enjoy serene boat rides on the Dunga Wetlands near Lake Victoria. The journey culminates in the world-famous Maasai Mara, where you will enjoy two full days of exceptional game viewing in one of Africa’s premier wildlife destinations.
itinerary: Nairobi:: Upon arrival at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, you will be met by our representative for transfer to your city hotel where you can relax. Meals at own cost.

Naivasha:: After breakfast and check-out, you set off for Lake Naivasha, the first of the Rift Valley lakes, nestled at the foot of the majestic Mount Longonot. Lake Naivasha holds historical significance as the landing site of East Africa’s first air passengers, who arrived here aboard Imperial Airways’ flying boats. Following a leisurely lunch at the camp, you will embark on a charming afternoon boat ride across the lake. This is a perfect opportunity to observe the diverse waterbird species and spot hippos in their natural habitat.

Naivasha:: After breakfast, we head to Hell’s Gate, a small yet captivating national park situated in a volcanically active area. Here, you can hike and cycle while observing the diverse wildlife. Adventurers can also descend into the Ol Njorowa Gorge for a memorable trek through breathtaking scenery. Named for the intense geothermal activity within its borders, Hell’s Gate National Park is a remarkable part of the Great Rift Valley. Towering cliffs, water-carved gorges, rugged rock formations, bush-covered volcanoes, and rising plumes of geothermal steam create a magical atmosphere, making this park one of Africa’s most striking natural wonders.

Nakuru:: Today, you will journey to the enchanting Lake Nakuru National Park, arriving around midday. Nestled within the Great Rift Valley, this park is famed for its vast flocks of pink flamingos that once transformed the lake into a shimmering sea of color. Although the water level is now higher due to climate change, leading to fewer flamingos, the park still offers incredible wildlife experiences—especially the chance to see one of the endangered Big Five: the magnificent rhinoceros. After checking in, you’ll enjoy a delicious lunch before setting out on an exciting afternoon game drive. Along the lake’s edge, you may spot waterbucks, reedbucks, and graceful zebras. Lake Nakuru is home to both black and white rhinoceroses. The story of the black rhino is particularly moving: in 1987, only two individuals remained in the wild. Thanks to the establishment of a rhino sanctuary within the park and a successful breeding program with a partner from Laikipia, the Kenya Wildlife Service managed to bring the population back from the brink of extinction.

Lake Baringo National Park:: After an early breakfast, you set off northwards towards the enchanting twin lakes of the Great Rift Valley: Baringo and Bogoria. Your first stop is at the Lake Bogoria National Reserve, a saline volcanic lake renowned as an important breeding ground for numerous bird species. Up to two million pink flamingos call this place home, feeding on the algae that flourish in the water and painting the lake in vibrant hues. Besides these magnificent flamingos, you can also spot bee-eaters, kingfishers, hornbills, and many other bird species. But the wildlife here is not limited to birds: large herds of the rare Greater Kudus roam the area, a sight seldom seen in East Africa. Buffalo, zebras, dik-diks, impalas, warthogs, vervet monkeys, and baboons also inhabit this diverse ecosystem, adding to the breathtaking natural spectacle.

Lake Baringo National Park:: After breakfast, you will explore Lake Baringo, the second-largest freshwater lake in the Rift Valley—surpassed only by Lake Naivasha—and the highest at 970 meters above sea level. Water flows gently into the lake from the Mau and Tugen Hills, creating a unique habitat that supports more than 450 species of birds and animals. Many of the migratory waterbirds here hold significant regional and global importance. You will enjoy a boat ride on the lake to enjoy the birdlife.

Kerio Valley National Park:: This morning after breakfast, depart Lake Baringo and travel into the dramatic Kerio Valley, one of Kenya’s most breathtaking escarpment landscapes. Your base for the night is Kerio View perched high on the rim with sweeping panoramas of the valley floor far below. The highlight of the day is a guided visit to Rimoi National Reserve, where you will explore the acacia-dotted plains and riverine forests in search of elephants, buffaloes, and various antelope species. Lunch is included during the reserve excursion, allowing you to picnic while soaking in the scenery. Return to the lodge in the late afternoon for a leisurely evening and dinner overlooking the valley. Game drive in Rimoi National Reserve

Kisumu:: After breakfast, continue westward to the lakeside city of Kisumu on the shores of Lake Victoria. You will check into the comfortable Sarova Imperial hotel. After breakfast, set off for a full-day excursion to Kakamega Forest, Kenya’s only true tropical rainforest. Guided walks through the lush canopy will reveal colobus monkeys, hornbills, and a dazzling variety of butterflies and orchids. You will also enjoy a treetop canopy walkway. Return to Kisumu and unwind at the hotel. Kakamega Forest hike and treetop canopy walk

Kisumu:: After a relaxed breakfast, head out to the nearby Dunga Wetlands for a peaceful boat ride through the papyrus-fringed channels of Lake Victoria. This wetland sanctuary is excellent for birdwatching, with chances to see African fish eagles, jacanas, and possibly hippos in the shallow waters. The rest of the day is flexible, allowing you to enjoy the hotel’s pool, spa, or lakeside gardens.

Masai Mara National Reserve:: Today, we continue our thrilling safari adventure in the magnificent Maasai Mara Game Reserve—a place whose name means “spotted plains” in the Maasai language. This breathtaking reserve is home to the largest concentration of big mammals in Africa and is widely regarded as one of the continent’s ultimate wildlife sanctuaries. The vast savannahs and gentle rolling hills provide an unparalleled backdrop for the incredible array of wildlife living here in their natural habitat. The Maasai Mara Game Reserve is also the northern extension of the vast Serengeti-Mara ecosystem, world-famous for the spectacular annual Great Migration. This awe-inspiring natural phenomenon sees thousands of wildebeest, zebras, and gazelles journeying across the plains in search of fresh grazing grounds.

Masai Mara National Reserve:: After an early breakfast, you will embark on exciting game drives both in the morning and the afternoon, exploring the captivating Maasai Mara Game Reserve. The landscape of the reserve is predominantly open savannah, consisting of vast grasslands, with iconic acacia trees dotting the southeastern parts of the park. Gently rolling hills are traversed by the winding Mara and Talek rivers, adding a dynamic beauty to the scenery. During the rainy season, numerous seasonal rivers flow through the area, transforming the landscape into a vibrant network of waterways. However, these rivers gradually dry up once the rains cease, creating a fascinating cycle that shapes the vegetation and wildlife behavior throughout the year.

End of Itinerary:: This morning after breakfast you will travel back to Nairobi where you will be dropped at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport for your flight home. Transfer to Jomo Kenyatta International Airport

touren.name: 14 Days Lake Victoria Three-Country Circuit
touren.desc: This unique 14-day journey takes you on a full circuit around Lake Victoria, combining remote islands, iconic wildlife, and authentic cultural encounters across three countries.
itinerary: Nairobi:: Arrive in Nairobi and check into the Trademark Hotel on a bed-and-breakfast basis. After clearing immigration and collecting your luggage you will be met by your driver for the short transfer to the hotel. Settle into your room, perhaps enjoy a swim or a massage at the spa, and take a gentle stroll through the hotel gardens or nearby shopping area if your flight lands early enough. Dinner is at your own leisure—Nairobi has excellent restaurants nearby, or you can dine at the hotel.

Mfangano Island:: Depart early morning to Mbita / Rusinga Island, Then take the boat and cruise across Lake Victoria to Mfangano Island and Mfangano Camp. The short, scenic boat ride offers wonderful birdwatching and perhaps a glimpse of hippos and crocodiles. Upon arrival at the intimate Mfangano Camp you will be welcomed with a refreshing drink and shown to your private cottage. Spend the day discovering the island’s ancient rock art, visiting a local fishing village, or joining a guided fishing excursion on the lake. Return to camp for a sunset drink on the deck and a superb dinner featuring fresh lake fish.

Mfangano Island:: Transfer by boat from Mfangano Island to Takawiri Island and Victoria Sands on a full-board basis. The morning transfer is relaxed and picturesque, with time to spot more of Lake Victoria’s wildlife en route. At Takawiri you settle into your luxurious beachfront accommodation and enjoy the private-island atmosphere—swim in the clear waters, lounge on the white-sand beach, or take a guided nature walk through the island’s forests. Afternoon options include a sunset dhow cruise or simply unwinding with a book and a cocktail. Dinner is served on the beach or on your private deck.

Lake Victoria, Kenya:: Tramsfer by boat to Rusinga Island. Once on the water, the ride to the island is filled with views of fishermen, water birds and the distant hills. Check into your accommodation at Victoria Sands (near Ruma National Park) and enjoy a leisurely lunch. In the afternoon, you can explore Ruma National Park in search of the rare roan antelope and black rhino (on extra costs), or simply relax on the beach with a cold drink. Dinner is served overlooking the lake. Ruma National Park Game drive in the afternoon

Ikoma:: Drive to the Migori–Tarime border and cross into Serengeti National Park, continuing into the northern Serengeti. The Serengeti is one of Africa’s most iconic wildlife destinations, famed for its vast plains and the Great Migration, where thousands of wildebeest and zebra move in search of fresh grazing.

Ikoma:: On the second day in Serengeti National Park, enjoy extended game drives across the vast plains. In the northern Serengeti in particular, following the river systems often leads to large herds of wildebeest and zebr, depending on the season, you may even witness a dramatic river crossing. You’ll also have excellent chances to see predators such as lions and cheetahs in action, while giraffes, elephants, and a variety of antelope species complete the classic savannah scenery.

Lake Victoria, Tanzania:: Mwanza is beautifully set on the southern shores of Lake Victoria and is known for its striking granite rock formations that give the city a dramatic and distinctive landscape. As a lively port town, Mwanza offers an authentic glimpse into daily life on the lake, from bustling markets to traditional fishing boats lining the shoreline. A highlight of any visit is a boat tour on Lake Victoria. Gliding past small islands and unique rock outcrops, you experience the lake from a completely different perspective—especially in the early morning or at sunset, when fishermen head out in wooden boats and the light reflects softly on the water.

Burigi Chato National Park:: Drive to the shores of Lake Victoria and take the ferry to Rubondo Island National Park, a remote and untouched island paradise. Upon arrival, settle into the simple but scenic TANAPA bandas, surrounded by forest and overlooking the lake. During your stay, enjoy a guided bush walk through the island’s lush wilderness, where you may encounter sitatunga antelope, monkeys, and a rich variety of birdlife. Rubondo offers a truly off-the-beaten-track experience, combining raw nature, tranquility, and unique wildlife found nowhere else in Tanzania.

Burigi Chato National Park:: A safari in Rubondo Island National Park is very different from the classic savannah experience and feels much more remote and intimate. The island is covered in dense forest, making it ideal for guided walks rather than traditional game drives. Wildlife highlights include sitatunga antelope, which are specially adapted to the island’s swampy areas, as well as bushbuck, vervet monkeys, and the famous chimpanzees that were introduced here as part of a conservation project. While sightings of chimps are not guaranteed, the island is one of the few places in Tanzania where they exist in the wild.

Africa:: Enjoy a homestay experience in Biharamulo District, offering a rare opportunity to connect with local communities and gain insight into traditional ways of life. Spend time with the hosts, learn about daily routines, food preparation, and farming practices, and experience genuine Tanzanian hospitality. During your stay, you will also be introduced to the culture and traditions of the Haya people, one of the main tribes in this region. Through storytelling, music, and shared activities, you gain a deeper understanding of their heritage, beliefs, and close connection to the land.

Lake Victoria, Uganda:: Cross the border into Uganda and continue to Masaka, a lively town set in the rolling hills of southern Uganda. Known for its fertile landscapes and welcoming atmosphere, Masaka offers a glimpse into rural Ugandan life, with local markets, small farms, and vibrant roadside scenes along the way.

Ssese Islands:: Take the ferry across Lake Victoria to the Ssese Islands and continue to Brovad Sands Lodge, set on a beautiful stretch of sandy beach surrounded by tropical forest. Spend two relaxing days here, enjoying the laid-back island atmosphere.

Ssese Islands:: During your stay, you can unwind on the beach, swim in the lake, or explore the island on guided walks through the forest. A range of optional activities is available, including mountain biking, canoeing, visits to nearby caves, a town tour, quad biking, tricycle rides, or jet skiing—allowing you to tailor your stay to be as active or relaxed as you wish.

End of Itinerary:: After your island stay, return by ferry to the mainland and continue by road to Entebbe for your onward flight home. Enjoy the scenic drive through Uganda’s lush countryside, arriving in time for departure from Entebbe International Airport. Please enter your email address and/or telephone number.
